decks.notes: This deck focuses on assembling the Greasefang Parhelion combo, which includes cards like Greasefang, Okiba Boss and Parhelion II. The strategy involves using cards like Fatal Push and Guardian of New Benalia for control, while setting up with Monument to Endurance and Iron-Shield Elf. The deck also utilizes cards like Fleeting Spirit and Sheltered by Ghosts for protection. The land base includes various utility lands like Eiganjo, Seat of the Empire and Geier Reach Sanitarium. Overall, the deck aims to quickly assemble its combo pieces while disrupting the opponent's game plan with targeted removal and protection spells.

This Dimir Aggro deck focuses on quickly applying pressure on the opponent with efficient creatures and disruptive spells. With a mix of cheap removal like Fatal Push and disruption like Thoughtseize, the deck aims to control the board while steadily chipping away at the opponent's life total. Kaito, Bane of Nightmares and Moon-Circuit Hacker provide powerful threats that can quickly close out the game, while cards like Unholy Annex/Ritual Chamber help to maintain card advantage. Overall, this deck excels at applying early pressure and disrupting the opponent's game plan.

The deck revolves around assembling and protecting the combo pieces, Greasefang and Parhelion II, to create a powerful board presence and overwhelm the opponent. By using cards like Cache Grab and Multiversal Passage to search for key pieces, as well as Formidable Speaker and other creatures to provide additional support, the deck aims to quickly establish a dominant position on the battlefield. Bitter Triumph and other removal spells help to control the opponent's board, while cards like Thundering Broodwagon and Esika's Chariot offer additional threats and versatility to the deck's strategy.

The key strategy of this UW Control deck is to manipulate the game by countering opponent's spells, drawing cards, and controlling the board with removal spells. The deck includes a mix of counterspells like Dovin's Veto, card draw like Consult the Star Charts, and removal like Erode. It also features powerful planeswalkers like Teferi, Hero of Dominaria and Elspeth, Storm Slayer to generate card advantage and deal with threats. The deck aims to control the game until it can establish a strong board presence and secure the win.

The key concept of this deck is to utilize cheap spells with the ability to draw cards and discard cards, allowing the player to quickly fill their graveyard with Arclight Phoenix and then bring them back to the battlefield for a powerful aggressive strategy. By efficiently cycling through the deck while also dealing damage to the opponent, this deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with a fast and relentless assault, utilizing the synergy between spells and creatures to maintain pressure and secure victory.

The focus of this deck is to control the game through various means such as counterspells, removal, and card advantage. It aims to disrupt the opponent's strategy while setting up its own win condition. The deck includes a mix of powerful planeswalkers like Teferi, Hero of Dominaria and Narset, Parter of Veils, as well as versatile spells like Supreme Verdict and Lightning Helix. By managing the board state and limiting the opponent's options, the deck looks to outlast and outmaneuver its opponent to secure victory.

This deck focuses on utilizing aggressive creatures and burn spells to quickly reduce the opponent's life total. Monastery Swiftspear, Emberheart Challenger, and Soul-Scar Mage provide early aggression, while Kumano Faces Kakkazan and Screaming Nemesis offer late-game reach. Mutavault and Ramunap Ruins provide additional damage sources, while Reckless Rage and Burst Lightning help deal with opposing threats. Den of the Bugbear and Sokenzan, Crucible of Defiance offer additional utility and resilience. The strategy revolves around applying constant pressure and finishing off the opponent before they can stabilize.

This UR Aggro deck focuses on quickly casting low-cost spells to pump up creatures like Monastery Swiftspear and Soul-Scar Mage, while also maintaining control through cards like Reckless Rage and Boomerang Basics. Efficiency in card draw with Sleight of Hand and Experimental Synthesizer helps keep the aggression going, with Burst Lightning and Flow State providing direct damage to opponents. Den of the Bugbear acts as a powerful finisher, while the mana base allows for consistent access to both colors needed for this fast-paced strategy.

This aggressive red deck focuses on dealing quick and efficient damage to the opponent. Utilizing direct damage spells like Burst Lightning and Reckless Rage, along with creatures like Monastery Swiftspear and Soul-Scar Mage, this deck aims to overwhelm the opponent early on. With versatile creatures like Mutavault and Sunspine Lynx, along with powerful enchantments like Monstrous Rage, this deck can quickly close out games. Ramunap Ruins provides additional reach, while Sokenzan, Crucible of Defiance offers a powerful late-game threat. Overall, this deck prioritizes speed and pressure to secure victories.

This mono black control deck focuses on utilizing graveyard interactions and disruption spells to control the game. It includes powerful creatures like Sheoldred, the Apocalypse and Graveyard Trespasser, along with removal spells like Fatal Push and Go for the Throat. The deck also has hand disruption with cards like Thoughtseize and Duress to disrupt the opponent's game plan. Additionally, it has card advantage engines like Castle Locthwain and Liliana of the Veil to generate value. Overall, the deck aims to control the game by disrupting the opponent's strategy and gaining incremental advantages.

The main idea of this deck is to overwhelm the opponent with aggressive creature plays and direct burn spells. By utilizing fast creatures like Monastery Swiftspear and Soul-Scar Mage, backed up by powerful burn spells like Burst Lightning and Reckless Rage, the goal is to deal as much damage as possible quickly to defeat the opponent before they can stabilize. The inclusion of Kumano Faces Kakkazan and Monstrous Rage adds additional burn and creature damage to push through the opponent's defenses. Additionally, cards like Ramunap Ruins and Mutavault provide consistent damage sources throughout the game.

The main concept is to aggressively attack the opponent with a combination of creatures and burn spells while disrupting their hand and board with discard spells. The deck aims to quickly close out games by overwhelming the opponent with efficient threats and removal spells. The mana base is built to support the aggressive strategy by providing consistent colored mana sources. The inclusion of graveyard interaction and versatile creatures like Sheoldred, the Apocalypse and Kroxa, Titan of Death's Hunger adds further depth to the deck's game plan.

The primary focus of this UW Control deck is to control the game through a combination of counterspells, card draw, and removal. By utilizing cards like Dovin's Veto, Teferi, Hero of Dominaria, and March of Otherworldly Light, the deck aims to disrupt the opponent's strategy while maintaining card advantage. The inclusion of cards like Ultima and Emeritus of Ideation provide additional win conditions, complementing the control elements of the deck. The mana base is designed to support the deck's control strategy, with lands like Floodfarm Verge and Hallowed Fountain ensuring consistent access to the necessary colors of mana.

This Pioneer aggro deck focuses on overwhelming the opponent with fast creatures and burn spells. With cards like Soul-Scar Mage and Reckless Rage, you can control the board while dealing damage quickly. Monastery Swiftspear and Emberheart Challenger provide early pressure, while Kumano Faces Kakkazan and Monstrous Rage can finish off the opponent. The deck also benefits from the versatility of Mutavault and the direct damage of Burst Lightning. By maintaining pressure and utilizing burn spells effectively, this deck aims to win the game before the opponent can stabilize.

The main focus of this deck is to control the game through a combination of countering opponent's spells, removing threats from the board, drawing cards, and eventually overwhelming the opponent with powerful spells and creatures. By utilizing a mix of instant speed removal, card draw, and powerful finishers like Torrential Gearhulk and Jeskai Revelation, the deck aims to disrupt the opponent's game plan while building up its own resources and finishing the game with a strong, decisive play.

This deck focuses on utilizing spirit creatures and tempo spells to quickly overwhelm the opponent and control the board. The key strategy involves using cards like Skyclave Apparition and Exalted Sunborn to control the opponent's creatures, while utilizing tempo spells like No More Lies and Daydream to disrupt their strategy. By maintaining a fast and aggressive pace, the deck aims to pressure the opponent and secure victory before they have a chance to stabilize. The inclusion of cards like Quantum Riddler and Starfield Shepherd provide additional support and synergy with the spirit theme.
